text.title: ab-testing-basics Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for ab-testing-basics as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Running analysis before the pre-committed sample size is reached.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Peeking inflates false positives — the test is not valid until the pre-registered sample and end date are both met.

failure-mode.description: Testing 5 segments and calling the one that wins the "result".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Multiple comparisons without Bonferroni correction produces a ~23% family-wise false-positive rate at 5 tests.

failure-mode.description: Peeking at interim results and stopping early when a winner appears.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Inflates false positives 5–10x; the test is only valid at the pre-committed sample size and end date.

failure-mode.description: Testing multiple simultaneous changes in one variant.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Cannot isolate which change caused the effect; the test produces an uninterpretable compound result.

failure-mode.description: Ignoring guardrail metrics after a primary win.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pyrrhic victories — e.g. a signup-rate win that increases D7 churn — destroy more value than they create.
